preamble: The phonetic transformation from the diphthong /ei/ to /ui/ involves a significant shift in the quality of the initial vowel sound. This change is characterized by the first element of the diphthong moving from a mid-front unrounded position to a high-back rounded position, while the second element, /i/, remains constant. This type of shift is often observed in specific morphological contexts within a language.
phoneticEffects: Raising, Backing, and Rounding of the initial vowel element of the diphthong
sources: McClure, J. D. (1998). An Introduction to Modern Scots. Edinburgh University Press.
